articles.title: Berkshire Hathaway Makes A Bold Return to Delta Airlines With A $2.6bn Stake On its shoulders — What It Means For Buffett’s Legacy And the U.S Industries? The Answer Lies Ahead
articles.summary: Berkshire Hathaway has disclosed a new $2.6 billion stake in Delta Air Lines, marking its first major re-entry into the airline sector since exiting during the pandemic. This move, made under new CEO Greg Abel, signifies a potential shift in Berkshire's portfolio strategy and reflects confidence in Delta's market position and the travel industry's recovery. The investment reignites debate about Berkshire's cyclical relationship with airlines and will be closely watched as an indicator of new leadership's investment philosophy.

articles.title: Does DHT Holdings (DHT) Prioritize Earnings Strength Over Fleet Expansion After a Robust Quarter?
articles.summary: DHT Holdings reported robust Q1 2026 results, with revenue rising to US$186.48 million and net income to US$164.53 million, delivering an EPS of US$1.02. This strong performance was attributed to a vigorous spot market and ongoing fleet renewal. While the company's investment narrative is bolstered by efficient cash generation from volatile crude tanker cycles and a modernizing VLCC fleet, management expressed caution regarding further expansion despite a supportive balance sheet, focusing instead on earnings strength.
articles.content: DHT Holdings reported robust Q1 2026 results, with revenue rising to US$186.48 million and net income to US$164.53 million, delivering an EPS of US$1.02. While the company's investment narrative is bolstered by efficient cash generation from volatile crude tanker cycles and a modernizing VLCC fleet, management expressed caution regarding further expansion despite a supportive balance sheet, focusing instead on earnings strength.

articles.title: Devon Energy Jumps on Friday, Faces Investors’ Next Move Monday
articles.summary: Devon Energy Corp. (NYSE: DVN) shares surged 4.76% on Friday, closing at $49.49, driven by rising crude prices and the completion of its merger with Coterra Energy. The company announced an $8 billion buyback authorization and a quarterly dividend increase to $0.320 per share, while analysts raised price targets following the merger. Investors are now watching for Monday's trading session to see if the stock can maintain its gains amidst oil price fluctuations and broader market trends.

articles.title: Canadian Natural Resources announces quarterly dividend amid market decline
articles.summary: Canadian Natural Resources Ltd (CNQ) saw its stock rise by 3.01% despite a broader market downturn, following the announcement of a quarterly dividend of CAD 0.625 per share. This dividend, reflecting stable cash flow and profitability, coupled with a reaffirmed full-year outlook and a strong non-GAAP EPS for Q1, is enhancing investor confidence and attracting long-term interest. Analysts have mixed views, with some upgrading CNQ due to its robust financial health and increasing shareholder returns.

articles.title: News Corp Class A stock (US65249B2007): earnings, AI bets and streaming strategy in focus
articles.summary: News Corp Class A has been transforming from a traditional media group into a digital, subscription, and data-focused business, driven by strong growth in digital subscriptions at Dow Jones. The company is strategically focused on monetizing professional data products and expanding its digital real estate services, while also managing cyclical advertising demands and interest-rate sensitivities. US investors are exposed to a blend of financial information, news, real estate, and publishing, with the stock reflecting both strong digital growth and traditional market risks.

articles.title: Starbucks Stock Faces Monday Breakout Test After $400 Million Layoff Shock
articles.summary: Starbucks announced plans to cut 300 U.S. corporate jobs and close several regional support offices as part of CEO Brian Niccol's "Back to Starbucks" turnaround strategy. The company anticipates $400 million in restructuring charges, largely non-cash, but still reported strong fiscal second-quarter sales growth. Investors are watching to see if these cost-cutting measures signal financial discipline or underlying strain as the stock heads into a crucial Monday trading session.

The company anticipates $400 million in restructuring charges, largely non-cash, but still reported strong fiscal second-quarter sales growth. Investors are watching to see if these cost-cutting measures signal financial discipline or underlying strain as the stock heads into a crucial Monday trading session.
